title: Integrating Virtual, Mixed, and Augmented Reality to HRI Applications Using Game Engines
summary: New article in 'Applied Sciences' identifies accessible software tools for developing VR, AR, and MR experiences in HRI. The review classifies XR applications across social robotics, industrial programming, and teleoperation.
subtitle1: The Role of Game Engines in Robotics
description1: The study highlights how game engines like Unity and Unreal Engine have emerged as cost-effective and flexible solutions for developing 3D environments and advanced user experiences for the latest XR hardware.
description2: Research articles are categorized into four primary application groups: social robotics, end-user programming of industrial robots, teleoperation in inaccessible environments, and physical Human-Robot Collaboration (HRC).
highlight: The acceptance and success of XR technologies in HRI depend on both system efficiency and human-centered factors, including economic accessibility and the usability of software and hardware elements.
description3: The review identifies key communication tools such as ROS and rosbridge_suite, alongside interaction frameworks like Vuforia, ARCore, and the Mixed Reality Toolkit, which enable seamless two-way data flow between physical robots and virtual systems.
